name: Gram's Dinner Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 cake yeast or 1 packet dry yeast
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the hot milk, shortening, sugar, and salt in a large bowl. Mix until all ingredients are well combined; do not worry if the shortening doesn't fully melt.
2. Cool the mixture to lukewarm temperature.
3. Add the yeast and mix thoroughly.
4. Add the beaten eggs and mix until smooth.
5. Gradually add the flour, mixing continuously to form a smooth, soft dough. The dough should be very soft and sticky but not require kneading.
6. Let the dough rise until it has doubled in volume.
7. Gently punch down the dough and refrigerate until cold.
8. Turn the chilled dough out onto a floured pastry cloth and briefly knead.
9. Divide the dough into thirds. Roll out one third at a time, aiming for a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
10. Brush the rolled-out surface with melted butter.
11. Cut the circle of dough into 8 pie-shaped wedges.
12. Roll up each wedge, starting at the wide end, to form crescent rolls. Ensure the pointy end is on the bottom so the roll holds its shape during rising and baking.
13. Brush the tops of the rolls with melted butter.
14. Cover the rolls loosely and let them rise until nearly doubled in size.
15. Bake at 350°F for 16 minutes.
Note: These are fairly large rolls. If you prefer smaller rolls, divide the dough into quarters instead of thirds before rolling.
